items.q: What is appropriation in postmodern art?
items.correct: Deliberately reusing existing images or styles, often to comment on originality.
items.why: Students moralise about copying. Postmodern appropriation (Sherrie Levine, Richard Prince) is a CRITICAL STRATEGY - it questions authorship and originality, central modernist values.

items.q: Which colours typically advance in a composition?
items.why: Students conflate darkness with advancement. In atmospheric perspective, COOLER colours recede (think distant blue mountains) and warmer colours advance, regardless of darkness.

items.q: Why is understanding patronage important for studying historical art?
items.correct: Patrons commissioned art for specific purposes, shaping content and form.
items.misconception: Artists worked freely - patronage was irrelevant to the artwork.
items.why: Students romanticise the lone artist. Most pre-modern art was COMMISSIONED, with patrons specifying subject, materials, even composition. Understanding the commission illuminates choices the artist made.

items.q: Why does Islamic art use lots of pattern?
items.why: It's Avoids figure imagery. “No pigment” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: Day of the Dead is in which month?
items.why: Correct answer: November. “January” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: On which day in November does Day of the Dead begin?
items.why: Exact answer: 1. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
